pre_registered_predictions.P1_gamma1_null: PASS: gamma=1 has zero sampled CHSH write, terrain delta, and later release difference in all scenarios.
pre_registered_predictions.P2_memory_persistence: PASS in stress_context: CHSH-written terrain persists into phase2 and changes next-phase release.
pre_registered_predictions.P3_challenge_memory: PASS weakly in stress_context: residual terrain memory continues to alter phase3 challenge release.
pre_registered_predictions.P4_localization: PASS: finite-shot conservative write survives only in stress_memory; normal/storage are filtered to zero.
pre_registered_predictions.P5_post_write_specificity: Matched classical replay reproduces post-write memory dynamics, so quantum-specificity is in the write eligibility, not in terrain decay itself.

safe_interpretation: A finite-shot sampled CHSH measurement-boundary signal can be written into terrain, persist after measurement stops, and weakly affect a later challenge phase in stress context. The post-write memory dynamics are classical terrain dynamics: a matched classical replay of the same write trajectory reproduces them. This is model-level positive for measurement-boundary terrain memory, not for quantum-specific post-write terrain physics or ordinary local population plumbing.
limitations: Not chemical realism or biological metabolism.
The result still uses simulated quantum states and simulated finite-shot CHSH measurements.
Post-write terrain memory is classical once the CHSH-excess write has occurred.
Local population plumbing without measurement-boundary writeback remains reduced-Arm2 reproducible.
